articles.title: House Appropriations Subcommittee Clears NASA Bill on Party-Line Vote
articles.summary: The House Appropriations Commerce-Justice-Science subcommittee approved the FY2027 bill that funds NASA today on a party-line vote, with all Democrats in opposition. Most of their objections were over parts of […]

articles.title: NASA Invites Media to Ireland Artemis Accords Signing
articles.summary: Ireland will sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at 3 p.m. EDT Monday, May 4, at NASA Headquarters in Washington. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman will host Ambassador of Ireland to the United States of America Geraldine Byrne Nason; Minister for Enterprise, Tourism and Employment Peter Burke, T.D., of Ireland; and U.S. Department of State […]

articles.title: Blue Origin certainly has ambitious launch targets for New Glenn
articles.summary: If Blue Origin wants to launch New Glenn 100 times a year, we're here for it.

articles.title: Odyssey Team Celebrates on a Global Map of Mars
articles.summary: Description Team members past and present from NASA’s 2001 Mars Odyssey orbiter mission gathered on April 15, 2026, to celebrate 25 years since the spacecraft’s launch, which took place April 7, 2001. For the occasion, the team rolled out a giant global map of Mars created using imagery from Odyssey’s THEMIS (Thermal Emission Imaging System) […]

articles.title: What’s Up: May 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
articles.summary: Shooting stars before dawn, a brilliant meetup between the Moon and Venus and a rare blue moon to end the month.

articles.title: Artemis III Moon Rocket Core Stage on the Move
articles.summary: Teams move the core stage, or largest section, of the SLS (Space Launch System) rocket for NASA’s Artemis III mission into the Vehicle Assembly Building at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida in this photo from April 27, 2026. The SLS core stage traveled 900 miles on the Pegasus barge from NASA’s Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans where the stage is manufactured, to complete […]

articles.title: NASA Welcomes Morocco as 64th Artemis Accords Signatory
articles.summary: The Kingdom of Morocco signed the Artemis Accords on April 29th during a ceremony in the country’s capital, Rabat, becoming the latest nation to commit to the responsible exploration of space. “It is my privilege to welcome the Kingdom of Morocco as the newest signatory to the Artemis Accords,” said NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman in […]

articles.title: NASA Goddard’s Greenbelt Visitor Center Marks 50th Anniversary
articles.summary: Trimmed in bicentennial pageantry, NASA opened a visitor center at its Goddard campus in Greenbelt, Maryland, in May 1976. Fifty years on, the Goddard Visitor Center continues to inspire through exhibits and programs on the past, present, and future of space exploration. When the visitor center first opened its doors (just a few weeks before […]

articles.title: NASA Explores Prioritizing First Response Drones in Crowded Skies
articles.summary: Our streets are crowded with commuters and delivery vehicles, but when a police car or fire engine approaches with its lights and sirens on, drivers clear the way. In the coming years, drones for deliveries and other commercial tasks will become common in the skies over our communities, and NASA is working to ensure first […]
